evals.expected_output: Skill fetches MR metadata, searches for a JIRA ticket in the title/description, fetches diffs, attempts to locate the local clone and create an isolated review worktree pinned to the MR's head_sha, reviews across all five Code Review Pyramid layers (invoking the code-review-pyramid skill if available), posts inline comments via scripts/post_review_notes.py (each ending with 'Co-reviewed with :robot:'), removes the worktree, and outputs the review summary in the conversation (does NOT post it to the MR). Does NOT approve or submit the review.
expectations: Inline comments are posted via scripts/post_review_notes.py rather than hand-written glab api calls
Each inline comment ends with the 'Co-reviewed with :robot:' footer
The review summary is output in the conversation, not posted to the MR
The conversation summary starts with '## Code Review Summary' and includes a Pyramid coverage table
The review covers Implementation Semantics and API Semantics (bottom layers)
The skill attempts to invoke the code-review-pyramid skill, or falls back to its own judgment if unavailable
The skill attempts to locate a local clone and create an isolated review worktree (never checking out the branch in the user's main clone), or notes the limitation if not found
The skill does not call any approve, submit, or review-completion API endpoint
If a JIRA ticket is found in the MR, the summary references it

evals.prompt: Can you do a code review on https://gitlab.com/acme/platform/-/merge_requests/7? There's no JIRA ticket for this one, it's a quick hotfix.
evals.expected_output: Skill reviews the MR without JIRA context. The conversation summary explicitly states no JIRA ticket was found. Review still covers all pyramid layers. Inline comments are posted where issues exist. Skill attempts to create an isolated review worktree locally rather than checking out the branch in place.
expectations: Conversation summary states no JIRA ticket was found
Inline comments are still posted (review proceeds without JIRA context)
Each inline comment ends with the 'Co-reviewed with :robot:' footer
The review summary is output in the conversation, not posted to the MR
The skill does not halt or ask for a ticket — it continues without one
The skill attempts to locate a local clone and create an isolated review worktree, or notes the limitation if not found

evals.prompt: Please review https://gitlab.com/acme/backend/-/merge_requests/201 — the ticket is GH-4521 in the MR title
evals.expected_output: Skill finds GH-4521 in the MR title, fetches the JIRA ticket details, and uses the acceptance criteria to evaluate whether the implementation satisfies the requirements. The conversation summary links to the JIRA ticket.
expectations: JIRA ticket GH-4521 is fetched and referenced in the summary
At least one inline comment references the JIRA requirements
The review summary is output in the conversation and links to the JIRA ticket
The review summary is not posted to the MR
Each inline comment ends with the 'Co-reviewed with :robot:' footer
